stem: Triangle ACD partially overlaps triangle EBD. Point C is on segment BD; point E is on segment AD. The measure of angle AEB is x°. In the figure, AC = CD, the measure of angle EBC is 51°, and the measure of angle ACD is 86°. What is the value of x?
rationale: Since AC = CD, triangle ACD is isosceles and the measures of ∠CDA and ∠CAD are equal. The three angles sum to 180°, so each base angle is (180 − 86)/2 = 47°. In triangle EBD, angle AEB is an exterior angle, so it equals the sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles: ∠AEB = ∠EBD + ∠EDB = 51 + 47 = 98°.
